title: SEISMOGRAPH: A Federated, Privacy-Preserving Early-Warning Network for Silent LLM/Agent API Drift
description: SEISMOGRAPH is an open-source, federated early-warning network that detects silent behavioural drift in third-party LLM and agent APIs. Lightweight, content-addressed canary probes (≤200 prompts at temperature 0) are replayed against target models; only SHA-256 hashes and differentially-private distributional aggregates (ε=2.0) leave the probe perimeter — raw prompts and outputs never do. A two-layer detector (Page-CUSUM change-point detection plus cross-observer quorum agreement) promotes a drift signal to a public alert only when ≥2 independent observers agree. On a reproducible reconstruction of the publicly documented Anthropic Claude 3.5 Sonnet degradation of August–September 2025, a single probe raised its first alert 38 days before the official postmortem, while the fault still affected only ~0.8% of traffic.
